english: To the one who wears the serpent king as garland, the three-eyed, smeared with sacred ash, the great Lord
roman: Nagendra haraya trilochanaya, bhasmanga ragaya Maheshwaraya

hindi: नित्याय शुद्धाय दिगम्बराय, तस्मै नकाराय नमः शिवाय॥
english: To the eternal, pure, sky-clad one — salutations to Shiva beginning with Na
roman: Nityaya Shuddhaya Digambaraya, tasmai Na karaya namah Shivaya

english: Anointed with Mandakini water and sandal paste, the great Lord, master of Nandi and the Pramatha hosts
roman: Mandakini salila chandana charchitaya, Nandishvara pramatha natha Maheshwaraya

english: Worshipped with Mandara and many flowers — salutations to Shiva beginning with Ma
roman: Mandara pushpa bahu pushpa supujitaya, tasmai Ma karaya namah Shivaya

english: To Shiva who is like the sun to the lotus-face of Gauri, who destroyed the sacrifice of Daksha
roman: Shivaya Gauri vadana aravinda suryaya, Dakshadhvara nashakaya

hindi: श्रीनीलकण्ठाय वृषध्वजाय, तस्मै शिकाराय नमः शिवाय॥
english: To the auspicious blue-throated one with bull as his emblem — salutations to Shiva beginning with Shi
roman: Shri Neelakanthaya vrisha dhvajaya, tasmai shi karaya namah Shivaya

english: Whose crest is worshipped by the greatest sages — Vasishtha, Agastya, Gautama and others
roman: Vasishtha kumbhodbhava Gautamarya, munindra devarchita shekharaya

english: Whose eyes are the moon, sun and fire — salutations to Shiva beginning with Va
roman: Chandraarka vaishwanara lochanaya, tasmai Va karaya namah Shivaya

english: This sacred five-syllable hymn, whoever reads it in the presence of Shiva
roman: Panchaksharam idam punyam yah pathet shiva sannidhau

english: Attains the realm of Shiva and rejoices with Shiva
